name: Prince Edward Island (Canada)

colors.symbolism: the gold field of the banner of arms
the lion in chief and the fringe blocks
the island and trees in the lower section
the fringe blocks alternating with red
the outlines of the trees and lion

description: A banner of the provincial arms: a gold field with a red lion passant in the chief, and on a green island below, an English oak flanked by three smaller oak saplings; the top, bottom, and fly are bordered by alternating red and white squares.
history: Adopted on 24 March 1964. The arms were granted to the Province by Royal Warrant on 30 May 1905; the design of the flag is essentially a banner of those arms with the addition of a heraldic fringe of red and white squares.

features.symbolism: A banner of the provincial arms of Prince Edward Island, fringed on three sides by alternating red and white squares.

features.role: banner of the provincial arms (in chief a red lion passant on gold, recalling Edward, Duke of Kent and Strathearn; below, on a green island, an English oak with three young oak saplings)
features.symbolism: The lion in chief recalls Prince Edward Augustus, Duke of Kent and Strathearn (father of Queen Victoria), after whom the island is named; the great oak represents Great Britain and the three saplings the three counties of the province.

features.role: fringe of alternating red and white squares along the top, fly, and bottom edges
features.symbolism: a heraldic fringe distinguishing the banner of arms; its red-and-white pattern echoes the arms's tinctures
